title: Principal Software Engineer - Agentic AI
company_stage: Series B/C (inferred from "3,250 customers", "rapid growth", founded 2017)
company_focus:
Infrastructure-as-code platform and cloud management with
AI-powered automation
description:
We're building an autonomous infrastructure engineering
agent: think Claude Code, but for your cloud. If you're
excited about agentic AI systems that solve impactful
problems for engineers, developers, and security experts at
innovative startups and iconic enterprises alike, this role
is for you.

Neo is Pulumi's answer to what infrastructure engineering
looks like in an AI-first era. It's a virtual infrastructure
teammate that understands your cloud environment, plans and
executes multi-step operations, and brings domain expertise
to problems ranging from spinning up complex ML
infrastructures to cloud migrations to continuous compliance
enforcement.

What makes Neo possible is infrastructure-as-code: by
modeling cloud infrastructure in code space, we can leverage
the fact that LLMs are already excellent at coding to affect
cloud infrastructure changes. Combined with Pulumi's
registry and petabytes of deployment history, we have a
unique foundation for building infrastructure intelligence.

We're looking for engineers to build all aspects of Neo:
from agent orchestration and tool interfaces to the web
experience where users watch Neo work, approve operations,
and understand its reasoning, to the interface with the
models and how we orchestrate and fine-tune them to maximize
impact. We seek bold thinkers to reimagine the entirety of
cloud engineering.

While Pulumi is headquartered in Seattle, WA., we are
dedicated to being a remote first company. This role will be
located remotely.

Responsibilities

- Build the planning and execution systems that let Neo safely
  perform multi-step infrastructure operations across AWS,
  Azure, GCP, Kubernetes, and more
- Design tool interfaces that give the agent semantic
  understanding of cloud resources and access to engineering
  workflows
- Develop the context systems that help Neo understand each
  customer's infrastructure patterns and usage
- Create the feedback loops and self-correction mechanisms
  that make autonomous operation reliable
- Build evaluation frameworks to measure agent performance
  across Neo's capability areas: migrations, compliance,
  cost optimization, diagnostics, and more
- Work on the web interface where Neo operates as a "virtual
  teammate"—task assignment, progress visibility, approval
  workflows
- Integrate Neo's capabilities into Pulumi's MCP Server for
  agent-to-agent workflows
- Collaborate with platform teams to deeply integrate agent
  capabilities into the CLI, SDKs, and Pulumi Cloud

You may be a good fit if you:

- Are genuinely excited about agentic AI—systems that plan,
  act, and adapt in the real world, not just generate text
- Have built production systems using LLMs, including
  experience with tool use, function calling, and multi-step
  orchestration
- Care deeply about reliability and safety when AI systems
  take real actions with real consequences
- Are comfortable across the stack—agent orchestration,
  backend services, and frontend interfaces
- Have strong Python and TypeScript skills (our primary
  languages) and can pick up new environments quickly
- Bring a product mindset: you think about what users need,
  not just what's technically interesting
- Communicate clearly and work well with a distributed team

Strong candidates may also have:

- Experience with cloud infrastructure (AWS, Azure, GCP) or
  infrastructure-as-code tools
- Background in building developer tools, CLIs, or platform
  engineering systems
- Familiarity with container orchestration (Kubernetes,
  Docker)
- Experience building evaluation frameworks and benchmarks for
  AI systems
- Knowledge of prompt engineering, fine-tuning, or retrieval-
  augmented generation

Compensation

- Base Salary: $190,000-220,000
- All full-time employee offers at Pulumi include base salary,
  bonus or variable, equity and benefits (details below).

Benefits & Perks at Pulumi:

Healthcare Coverage: We offer comprehensive medical, dental,
vision, and supplemental insurance at no cost to U.S.
employees. Internationally, we comply with local healthcare
requirements and provide regionally appropriate coverage.

Time Off: Our unlimited PTO policy encourages balance and
rest — and we require employees to take at least three weeks
off annually, plus 13 U.S. holidays.

401(k): U.S. employees are eligible for a 401(k) plan with
an employer match to support long-term financial wellness.

Parental Leave: We provide 20 weeks of paid leave for
birthing parents or primary caregivers, and 16 weeks for
non-birthing parents or secondary caregivers.

Remote-First Culture: Pulumi has been fully remote since
2020, with teammates across 20+ U.S. states and 12+
countries. We support flexible work with asynchronous
collaboration and an annual “work from anywhere” stipend.

Professional Development: Every employee receives an annual
learning and development budget to support growth, learning,
and career goals.

Equity Ownership: We believe in acting like owners. All
employees receive equity and are empowered to think big,
move fast, and build the future of cloud together.

Additional Support: We offer a monthly wellness fund to
support mental and physical well-being, and a quarterly
happiness fund for team connection.

About Pulumi:

Pulumi is reimagining how teams build cloud software,
enabling developers and infrastructure experts to work
better together through a unique combination of programming
languages, tools, and systems innovation.

Our flagship infrastructure as code technology is open
source and our SaaS product, Pulumi Cloud, provides platform
teams, secrets management, and cloud management
capabilities, and more. We have pioneered leveraging AI
across all of these areas with our LLM-powered Pulumi
Copilot to push the boundaries of what's possible. At
Pulumi, we dream big, in the pursuit of helping our
customers out-innovate and win.

Founded in 2017 by industry veterans with over five decades
of combined experience building developer platforms, Pulumi
now has a global community of more than 350,000 members and
serves over 3,250 customers. Despite our rapid growth, we're
still only getting started, and are early in our mission to
democratize the cloud. If you thrive in a fast-paced, high-
performance, we want to work with you to accelerate Pulumi's
impact.

At Pulumi, we don't just accept difference, we celebrate,
support, and thrive on it for the benefit of our employees,
our products, and our customers. Pulumi is proud to be an
equal opportunity workplace and is committed to equal
employment opportunities regardless of race, color,
ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual
orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability,
gender identity, or Veteran status. If you have a disability
or special need that requires accommodation, please let us
know.

ai_classification_reasoning:
This role involves building autonomous AI agents (Neo) that
plan, execute, and self-correct multi-step infrastructure
operations. The work includes agent orchestration, tool
interfaces, model orchestration and fine-tuning, and
evaluation frameworks for AI systems—all hands-on
development of agentic AI capabilities.
